content:
Over the past year, I've been building CODELIVLY, a cybersecurity learning platform focused on helping beginners and aspiring professionals learn by doing instead of just watching videos.

🔗 Website: https://codelivly.com

Why I Started

When I began learning cybersecurity, I found myself jumping between YouTube tutorials, blogs, PDFs, labs, and documentation.

Everything was scattered.

I wanted one place where someone could:

Learn the fundamentals

Practice real concepts

Track their progress

Earn certificates

Engage with a learning community

That idea eventually became CODELIVLY.

What the Platform Includes

The goal wasn't to build another course website.

Instead, I wanted to create an ecosystem for continuous learning.

Current features include:

📚 Structured learning paths

🎯 Career-focused roadmaps

🧪 Hands-on labs

📝 Interactive quizzes

🏆 Certificates

👥 Community discussions

🎮 Competitions

📖 Cybersecurity glossary

🛠️ Free cybersecurity resources and tools

Everything is designed to help learners progress from beginner concepts toward practical cybersecurity skills.

Tech Stack

The platform is built using a modern web stack, including:

Next.js

React

TypeScript

Node.js

PostgreSQL

Prisma

Tailwind CSS

A lot of effort also went into the admin dashboard for managing content, users, certificates, learning paths, analytics, and community features.

What I Learned

Building the platform taught me that software isn't just about writing code.

It's also about:

Designing intuitive user experiences

Structuring content effectively

Handling authentication and permissions

Building scalable admin tools

Listening to user feedback and iterating

The product has evolved significantly since the first version thanks to community feedback.

What's Next

I'm continuing to expand the platform with:

More practical labs

Additional career paths

More community features

Better learning analytics

Improved mobile experience

New free cybersecurity tools

I'd Love Your Feedback

If you have a few minutes, I'd really appreciate any feedback on:

Overall UI/UX

Learning experience

Navigation

Performance

Features you'd like to see

Anything that feels confusing or could be improved

Website:

👉 https://codelivly.com

Thanks for taking the time to check it out. Feedback from the developer community is incredibly valuable as I continue improving the platform.
